posts.name: Michael S. Monie
posts.cooked: I stopped buying it several hundred dollars a bottle ago. One hit on winesearcher in Hong Kong at over $500 a bottle. Even if it hasn’t hit the U.S. I expected to see it released in Europe.

posts.name: Sh@n Agra
posts.cooked: I am told the winery has asked for it to only be released in September; anything you see prior may be grey market
Rare Wine Co expecting it late fall/early winter. Would assume the same for east coast importers.

posts.name: Josh Grossman
posts.cooked:
Sorry. Fu drank it all already.

Corrected that for you

posts.name: Ian Dorin
posts.cooked: This always comes out October/November in the US.

posts.name: Todd Tucker
posts.cooked: What was said above. Maria Teresa releases the Barolo in September. It gets to the US later. Anyone selling it now is banking on their expected allocation to sell, but certainly does not have it in hand.

posts.name: Ofer Refaeli
posts.cooked: Out in October

posts.name: Jeff Fertitta
posts.cooked:
This is one of my fav wines. When I lived in bmore it was November, now that I’m in Boston I don’t see it until Dec/Jan.

I’m in for a case if I can actually put that together. Usually I can only get a handful of bottles at retail.
